issues.message: Test file reads template files from disk using readFileSync — real filesystem I/O in unit tests
issues.suggestion: Mock the fs module with vi.mock("fs") or memfs, or move these into a dedicated integration test category that is excluded from fast unit test runs

issues.message: vi.restoreAllMocks() is called in afterEach but vi.stubGlobal() is not undone by restoreAllMocks — only vi.unstubAllGlobals() correctly cleans up stubbed globals
issues.suggestion: Replace vi.restoreAllMocks() with vi.unstubAllGlobals() in afterEach, or add vi.unstubAllGlobals() alongside it, to ensure the global fetch stub does not leak between test files

issues.message: beforeEach manually deletes process.env keys after calling vi.stubEnv — mixing manual mutation with vi.stubEnv creates unpredictable env state; afterEach calls vi.unstubAllEnvs() which is correct, but the beforeEach delete is redundant and can interfere with Vitest internal env tracking
issues.suggestion: Remove the manual delete process.env[env] calls from beforeEach. vi.stubEnv with an empty string and vi.unstubAllEnvs() in afterEach is sufficient for clean isolation

issues.message: Non-null assertion operator (\!) used on test results inside expect chains — if the assertion before it fails, the non-null assertion will throw a TypeError rather than a clean assertion failure message
issues.suggestion: Use expect(configFile).toBeDefined() then a type-safe narrowing (e.g. if (\!configFile) throw new Error()) before accessing configFile.content, or use Vitest expect.assertions() to ensure the guard branch is hit
issues.evidence:
expect(configFile).toBeDefined();
const parsed = JSON.parse(configFile\!.content) as Record<string, unknown>;

issues.message: Mutation-killing tests are split into separate *-mutants.test.ts files co-located with primary test files, with no convention or documentation explaining the split — developers may not know which file to extend when adding new tests
issues.suggestion: Either consolidate mutant-killer tests into the primary test file (adding a comment block), or document the convention (e.g. in a README or test file header) so the pattern is explicit and consistent across the codebase
issues.evidence:
src/core/__tests__/parser.test.ts        (27 tests)
src/core/__tests__/parser-mutants.test.ts (57 tests)
src/core/__tests__/validator.test.ts      (27 tests)
src/core/__tests__/validator-mutants.test.ts (14 tests)

issues.message: No vitest.config.ts exists and the test script runs all tests together with no category separation — there is no way to run only fast unit tests (e.g. on save) separately from slower tests like templates.test.ts which performs real disk reads
issues.suggestion: Add a vitest.config.ts with workspace projects or include/exclude patterns. Define an npm script like "test:unit" that excludes real-I/O tests, enabling fast feedback on save. Tag-based filtering (e.g. vitest --project unit) would also allow CI to run categories in parallel
